注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換資訊請參考 此連結 [https://intad.doshisha.ac.jp/en/exchange/guidence.html] 及 Information Sheet [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Doshisha-Exchange-Program-2025-2026.pdf] 日語組： Japanese Language and Culture Module (Nichibun) List of Courses and Syllabuses [https://intad.doshisha.ac.jp/intad/en/exchange/curriculum.html] Courses are conducted in Japanese . Students are placed in classes based on the result of our Japanese language placement test regardless of Bachelor, Master or PhD. For Nichibun students who are placed in Japanese level 5 or higher are allowed to take courses in Japanese offered by faculties. However, Law School, Business School and Faculty of Engineering don't open any courses to Nichibun exchange students. Students in CJLC program: Law School, Business School and Institute for the Liberal Arts (ILA) don’t open any courses 一般組： Global Education Module (GEM，舊名CGE) List of Courses and Syllabuses [https://intad.doshisha.ac.jp/intad/en/exchange/cge_curriculum.html] The Global Education Module (GEM) offers inter-disciplinary courses all conducted in English , designed for international and Japanese students to foster a better understanding of Japan in a global context. GEM provides in-depth learning opportunities in different fields ranging from the humanities and social sciences to the natural and human sciences. Exchange students who intend to principally take courses on Japanese Culture, Society, and Nature in English offered by GEM are accepted as ‘GEM students’. [In contrast, exchange students who intend to study mainly Japanese language are accepted as "Nichibun students".) Courses offered by CGE are undergraduate level, if Master and PhD students understand it, we can accept students regardless of Bachelor, Master or PhD. GEM students are not allowed take courses offered by any Graduate Schools other than ILA (undergraduate level). Students in CGE program: Law School and Business School don’t open any courses Courses that are available vary depending on each student’s Japanese level. Also note that there are the courses that cannot be taken. Such include seminar courses, subjects offered by Faculty of Science and Engineering and Faculty of Life and Medical Sciences, and subjects not open to other faculty students. 該校僅開放大學部的課程供所有學生選讀，請研究生及博士生自行考量修課限制。 在具備相關領域之背景知識及選課標準的前提下，交換學生可跨領域選課，研究所學生可選修大學部課程。 最低修課限制：Students should take more than 7 classes (1 class = 90 minutes) at least per week. It is prescribed by law. Estimated cost of living: The average monthly living cost in Kyoto is around ¥120,000 including rent (at the very least 100,000JPY). Please keep this in mind when making plans to study in Japan. All exchange students need to pay ¥2,500/semester as Educational Support Fee to the Center for Global Education at the beginning of each semester. (The amount of fee is subject to change.) Insurance information [https://ois.doshisha.ac.jp/en/health/nhi.html] : All International students residing in Japan for more than 3 months are required to enroll in the insurance. 獎學金資訊，請參考 此連結 [https://intad.doshisha.ac.jp/intad/en/exchange/scholarships.html]

BBA program at Chulalongkorn Business School has limited capacity for Fall Semester. Exchange students majoring in Thai who wish to attend the Department of Thai in the Faculty of Arts are required to submit the results of a Thai language proficiency test. They must demonstrate strong fluency in all four language skills — listening, speaking, reading, and writing. Therefore, students applying to the Department of Thai are typically expected to provide a letter, document, or test result from their Thai Language Center or Thai Department certifying their proficiency in all four skills. We also recommend that the student write a Statement of Purpose (SOP) in Thai. This will allow the department to better understand student's proficiency in the language and assess their suitability for the program. Submitting the English Language test is optional. Please note that each program/faculty has its own requirements which all students have to abide to and they have to carefully go through these requirements. Some programs strictly require a language certificate but for some programs, language requirements are flexible. Thus, the OIA decided to recommend that all students have an IELTS or TOEFL score record as mentioned Study in arts, humanities & social sciences subject area, usually require a minimum score of 6.5 in overall band (Depends) with a minimum of 6.0 in each band. Study in physical science, life sciences, sciences & technology subject area, usually require a minimum score of 6.0 in overall band (Depends) with a minimum of 5.5 in each band. Exchange students are required to arrange their own insurance against illnesses and accidents covering the period of their stay in Chulalongkorn University. 生活花費：Total (USD) : 5,510；Accommodation : 2,100 (+ water & electricity fees)、Food : 1,500、Transportation : 500、Textbooks : 150、Insurance : 60、Miscellaneous : 1.200 (traveling, shopping, etc.) 簽證資訊請參考 此連結 [https://www.inter.chula.ac.th/one-stop-service/visa/]

注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 該校為科學／工業大學，請欲選填該校的學生，先行查詢該校是否有相對應系所。如欲跨系所選填者，請務必具備相關領域之背景知識及選課條件符合之證明，以避免交換入學申請被拒絕 該校交換入學申請文件中，有一項為Acceptance Letter from your Intended Academic Advisor at Tokyo Tech，須由您欲申請之東京工業大學指導教授簽署同意，因此請先確認該校是否有相符之系所和指導教授，以確保錄取交換推薦資格後可以順利向姊妹校提交交換入學申請文件 交換生計畫網頁，請參考 此連結 [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/acap https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/ysep] 及 Information Sheet [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Science-Tokyo-Information-Sheet-AY2025-.pdf] 、課程相關網頁，請參考 此連結 [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/course-information] 、交換生語言課程資訊，請參考 此連結 [https://js.ila.titech.ac.jp/~web/japanese.html?_gl=1*19aiifh*_ga*MTc0NjUwMDc1LjE2NDM3NjcwMTU.*_ga_VKBJ61GEPE*MTY5MjE1MTk3OC4xNjk3LjEuMTY5MjE1NDczNC4wLjAuMA..] 不開放申請之科系，請參考 此連結 [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/public-relations/education/relationship] Master's or PhD students are NOT allowed to take undergraduate courses/subjects/units. Students need to fulfill the requirements mentioned in the "Program Outline" on the website of each program. YSEP [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/ysep] / ACAP [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/acap] YSEP and ACAP are eligible for the former Tokyo Tech partner universities’ students and they are not permitted to take courses offered by the faculties/graduate schools of Medicine,Dentistry and Health Care Sciences. Indeed, they cannot be accepted by the faculty members of the faculties/graduate schools of Medicine, Dentistry and Health Care Sciences. 一般組： Young Scientist Exchange Program(YSEP) [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/ysep] Those with student status at their home university, enrolled in the following programs at the time they start research at Science Tokyo: Doctoral program Master's program Bachelor's program (at least two and a half years of study) This program is limited to about 40‐50 students per year (25 students per semester). Participants will be competitively selected by Tokyo Tech if the number of applications exceeds the number of slots available. Before beginning the online application process, applicants are required to contact a desired academic supervisor at Tokyo Tech directly, and obtain approval from him/her. On the online application form, applicants need to submit evidence such as an email message or a letter indicating that the Tokyo Tech professor agrees to be the applicant’s academic supervisor. The applicant can choose three faculty members. However, if all of them refuse to accept the student, the student will be asked to give up participation in the program. The program is open to students at Tokyo Tech partner universities who, at the time of YSEP participation, are enrolled in and have completed at least two and a half years of undergraduate studies. 日語組： Academic Cooperation Agreement Program (ACAP) [https://www.titech.ac.jp/english/international-student-exchange/prospective-students/non-degree/acap] Take course(s) offered at Science Tokyo (required): Mandated to take 6 classes (600 minutes) or more per week to maintain student visa status. Within the "Contact Period with the ACAP Specialized faculty member", applicants are required to contact an ACAP Specialized faculty member by directly referring to the list of the ACAP Specialized faculty members which will be provided by Tokyo Tech, and obtain their approval. Please do NOT contact outside of the set time of period. On the online application form, applicants need to submit evidence such as an email message or a letter indicating that the ACAP Specialized faculty member agrees to accept them. Note that there are some Departments with many applicants which may exceed their limit and not accept any. accommodation_info: 不保證住宿 ，詳情請參閱以下資訊： Students are welcome to apply for on-campus housing regardless of the term they are attending Cornell; however, be aware that on-campus housing is limited and not guaranteed. Fall semester: Finding housing can be very challenging. On-campus housing [http://living.sas.cornell.edu/live/apply/] is not available, and single-semester leases within commuting distance are difficult to secure. Spring semester: Housing may be available on-campus and is generally easy to find off-campus. 注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換學生資訊請參考 此連結 [https://abroad.globallearning.cornell.edu/incoming-exchange-students] 、 此連結 [https://abroad.globallearning.cornell.edu/incoming-students/faq] 及 Fact Sheet [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/24-25_Cornell_Fact_Sheet.pdf] ；課程資訊請參考 此連結 [http://www.cornell.edu/academics/fields.cfm] Academic Restrictions: Graduate programs, and Cornell University degree/certificate programs outside Ithaca, NY are not open to university-wide exchanges. Exchange students are unable to take courses in Computer Science (CS) and Information Science. Related subjects may be found in other departments (Electrical and Computer Engineering; Information Science, Systems, and Technology; or Operations Research and Information Engineering). While business classes are available, they are restricted and can be difficult to get into. Studio classes (architecture, art, design, and fashion) are typically not available to students through general exchange agreements. Courses associated with professional graduate degree programs (MBA, MEng, MPS, MPH, etc.) are not available to non-degree students; in general, students should not expect to take graduate (MA, MS, PhD) courses. Senior capstone courses as well as first year writing seminars are not available to exchange students. Graduate degree programs and professional colleges and schools (Law, Veterinary Medicine, SC Johnson College of Business, Cornell Tech, Weill Cornell Medical School) are not included in university-wide or undergraduate college-specific exchange agreements. Students must enroll in a minimum of 12 credits per semester during the academic year. Most exchange students enroll in 14-16 credits per semester. Exceeding 16 credits is not recommended. Students entering Cornell for a full academic or calendar year are expected to maintain good academic standing. Good academic standing for incoming exchange and visiting students is defined as: Earn a term GPA of 2.0 or higher in their first term at Cornell. Successfully completing a minimum of 12 academic credits. To complete course credits, students must receive a letter grade of “D-” or higher, OR an “S” in a class taken S/U. Failure to maintain good academic standing for your first term at Cornell will result in dismissal from the program. All students are enrolled in Cornell's Student Health Insurance [https://studenthealthbenefits.cornell.edu/about/health-insurance-policy-rationale] . This is mandatory and cannot be waived by international students unless they are covered by an acceptable U.S. employer-based health insurance plan. 生活費資訊請參考 此連結 [https://finaid.cornell.edu/cost-to-attend]

accommodation_info: Students can apply for student residences or look for a room on the private market. More information here [https://www.uni-mannheim.de/en/academics/coming-to-mannheim/exchange-students/housing/] .

Please note that availability may be limited in some departments. Students may have the option to take courses from another Faculty; however, the majority of courses must be in the Faculty of Arts and Science. As of 2026/2027AY, the following subjects are not available to incoming exchange students. The restrictions may apply to the entire subject or bot available for incoming exchange students (may apply to the entire subject or only the specific course): History (at the 300-level and above) Kinesiology and Health Studies (all levels) Political Studies (at the 300-level and above) Sociology (all levels) These subjects are highly demanded at Queen’s, therefore courses have limited spaces: Biology Computing and Information Science Economics English and Creative Writing Film and Media Life Sciences (Microbiology, Neuroscience, Physiology) Psychology Students are not permitted to enroll in courses offered by the Faculty of Law. It is also challenging to secure courses in politics, computing science, drama and music, economics, and life science. Students may receive some courses but should not expect to take the bulk of courses in these disciplines. Minimum academic workload per semester: 3 courses or 9 units of study Adjusting to Canadian academics [https://sass.queensu.ca/students/international/] All incoming exchange students are required to purchase the University Health Insurance Plan (UHIP) [https://quic.queensu.ca/uhip/] prior to course registration. Estimated cost of living [https://www.queensu.ca/ipo/incoming-exchange/costs]

注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換生計畫各項資訊請參考 此連結 [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/eng/studyabroad/exchangeprograms/] 、 Fact Sheet [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/assets/uploads/2025/02/25A_FACT_SHEETSophia-6.pdf] （僅供參考）、 Brochure [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/assets/uploads/2025/02/25A_ExchangeBrochure-6.pdf] 及 電視專訪 [https://youtu.be/Hjr5RwmqlQs] 該校兩種課程說明（一般組及日語組）請參考 此連結 [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/eng/studyabroad/exchangeprograms/course_info/] 選課資訊請見 此連結 [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/eng/studyabroad/exchangeprograms/application_info/registration/] The following courses are NOT open to Exchange Students. FLA core programs FST laboratory classes GPGS compulsory courses the foreign language courses (except Japanese language) Seminars Compulsory courses in General Studies, “Studies in Christian Humanism: For Others, with Others”, “Liberal Arts of the Body”, “Critical Thinking & Writing”, “Overview of Data Science”, “Thinking about Issues, Perspectives and Positionality”. Academic English courses under University-wide General Studies (Courses with registration codes that start from GSP and the numberings with ENG) In principle, all applicants of Japanese-taught programs are required to submit Japanese-Language Proficiency Test N1 certificate; in addition: Applicants who wish to enroll in the Graduate Program in Linguistics (言語学専攻) must submit proof of a JLPT N1 obtained within two years from the time of application . Applicants who wish to take the Japanese Language Education Course (日本語教育学コース) subjects in the Graduate Program in Linguistics (言語学専攻) must submit proof of a JLPT N1 score of 150 or higher . For those who are applying to other Japanese-taught graduate program, a higher level may be required for the breakdown score in addition to passing the N1 level. Applicants who apply for the Graduate Program in Global Studies are expected to have a higher English ability. Applicants who cannot fulfill the following minimum language requirement will be denied the entrance into the program: 100 on TOEFL iBT or 7.0 on IELTS (Academic) Exchange students should choose most courses from the departments/graduate program that he/she enrolled in. Master/PhD students: If they decided to take only undergraduate level courses when the application, please apply for undergraduate department. Exchange Students must register for more than 10 hours of class work per week (600 minutes, 1 hour being 60 minutes). This is namely 6 periods as 100 minutes constitute one period. Visa [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/eng/studyabroad/exchangeprograms/application_info/visa/] , Scholarship [https://piloti.sophia.ac.jp/eng/studyabroad/exchangeprograms/housinginfo_costs/scholarship/] , Video [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w426bsCwRDw&list=PLPmHXQbKIWTxi0QFyti3D6dUjtlt0Uf6d&index=3] Estimated Cost of Living [https://www.sophia.ac.jp/eng/admissions/exchangeprograms/housingInfo_costs/living_expenses.html] Meals: ¥30,000 - ¥60,000 per month Public Transportation: ¥5,000 - ¥10,000 per month Books: ¥30,000 - ¥50,000 per semester Personal Expenses: ¥30,000up per month Insurance [https://www.sophia.ac.jp/eng/admissions/exchangeprograms/application_info/insurance.html] : In principle, all exchange students have to join Japanese National Health Insurance after you arrive in Japan.

accommodation_info: Seezeit student services [https://www.seezeit.com/en/] is the largest provider of student accommodation in Konstanz. The rooms are usually in single or double occupancy apartments [https://www.seezeit.com/en/accommodation/halls-of-residence/] as well as in shared flats (WGs = Wohngemeinschaft). Every student, however, receives their own personal room. The WGs are usually shared by male and female students. Due to the extremely high demand, the rooms are assigned by lottery. The application for the dormitory room is part of the application for an exchange place at the University of Konstanz and is completed together. Remember to transfer the deposit of 800€ to Seezeit. Only then can your application for a dormitory be considered.

注意事項: 請留意，該校表示因無法評估未來疫情之影響，即使提供交換名額予本校，仍有可能會臨時取消交換學生計畫。 另， 陸港澳地區調升為橙色旅遊警示 [https://www.mac.gov.tw/News_Content.aspx?n=B383123AEADAEE52&sms=2B7F1AE4AC63A181&s=CA568B3D88264221] ，請欲選擇至該校交換的同學留意。 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力等規定為該校之最低門檻，同學需自行確認最新資訊，如語言能力及成績是否符合該校要求、查詢是否有足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識等，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。本表所提供之資訊如有與交換學校／國家／地區最新公告不符之處，申請學生應以交換學校／國家／地區之最新公告為準。 經甄選錄取獲得本校推薦資格之交換生，不得於取得該校入學許可後，要求變更已取得的入學許可條件；或者是交換申請被該校拒絕後，要求以另一種條件重新提出申請。如有以上情事者，將直接取消其錄取資格 歷年成績不得有不及格、不通過、停修及重修記錄。 部分學院不接受交換生申請，包含但不限於：瀚德學院、漢語文化學院。 交換生只能選擇相符階段（本科階段或研究生階段）的課程，不能跨階段選課。 由於課程安排，不建議畢業班同學選擇春季交換。 交換生資訊請見 招生說明 [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/港澳台交换生接收说明.pdf] 交換生每學期至少需選擇3-5門課，其中至少2門為所屬學院的專業課。 課程資訊請參考 此連結 [https://iso.bnu.edu.cn/en/pages?cid=378&pid=184] 非港澳臺學生申請時須提交HSK5級以上漢語水平考試證書，或者TOEFL 80 / IELTS 6.0以上英文檢定證明

注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換資訊請參考 此 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/international/campus/] 連結 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/international/welcome/exchange/] 、 系所一覽表 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/education/] 、 課程資訊 [https://webstation-koukai.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/] 、 語言課程 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/international/welcome/exchange/file/file_chart.pdf] 、 修課限制 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/international/welcome/exchange/file/guidelines_english.pdf] Undergraduate exchange students need to take at least six classes per week for one semester to satisfy the requirements of their visa. Graduate exchange students need to take at least one class. Belonging to a graduate school, choose one of the two paths mentioned below: Subject Study Path (for exchange students who wish to take Japanese language subjects, subjects related to Japan and a wide range of specialist subjects, rather than conducting research in their major field): While the student will belong to a graduate school, they are permitted to take subjects at undergraduate and graduate level without restriction. However, they are required to take at least 6 subjects per semester including at least 1 subject in the graduate school to which they belong. No research supervisor will be allocated to such students. In the case that the graduate school a student wishes to enroll in does not accept “Subject Study Path” exchange students, then the student must either choose another graduate school or belong to an undergraduate faculty instead. Research Focus Path (for exchange students who wish to focus on conducting research activities intheir major field): A student will be accepted if allocation of a research supervisor, based on the contents of the submitted Research Plan, is deemed possible. At the conclusion of their exchange, the student must submit a report outlining the results of their research. The student must enroll in at least 1 subject in the graduate school to which they belong. Undergraduate Exchange Students along with graduate exchange students who select the Subject Study Path must take at least 6 classes/semester (1 subject = 100 minutes/week, 600 minutes per week in total) to maintain eligibility for their student visa. Graduate exchange students who select research focus path will need to spend more time on research activities and therefore do not have to follow the above rule. However, we request they register for at least 1 class in the graduate school to which they belong. 生活費資訊，請參考 此連結 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/international/welcome/exchange/] (>Tuition and Other Fees > Life Expenses) 保險資訊，請參考 此連結 [https://www.kanagawa-u.ac.jp/international/welcome/exchange/]

Class placement is final and may NOT be changed. Online syllabus [https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/pathways-future/syllabus/] : The search is case-sensitive, so it is best to copy the course titles from the online pamphlet and paste in the search field. Please note that guidance on research is not provided to SKP graduate students. SKP students can select courses only from the course lists for each track/campus distributed to them at the start of the course registration period. Students will not be able to choose from among all of the courses for regular students offered in English. Minimum academic workload per semester: It is required by Japanese law that international students attend a minimum of 10 hours of classes per week, which means at least 7 sessions of RU classes per week (1 session = 90 minutes). Estimated [http://en.ritsumei.ac.jp/skp/academics/scholarships/] cost of [http://en.ritsumei.ac.jp/skp/academics/scholarships/] living [http://en.ritsumei.ac.jp/skp/academics/scholarships/] : 100,000 yen per month. Living expenses will vary depending on each student's individual lifestyle, but a list of estimated expenses can be found on the following page at Estimated Living Expenses [https://en.ritsumei.ac.jp/skp/academics/scholarships/] . Health Insurance: Students are required to join the National Health Insurance system while on the Study in Kansai Program. This insurance covers 70% of incurred medical fees. The annual premium is approximately 18,000 yen. To provide assistance with this payment, the Kyoto City International Foundation reimburses a portion of the National Health Insurance cost to international students living in Kyoto. More details regarding the National Health Insurance system and applicable rebates will be provided during orientation. It is required by Japanese law (Emigration and Immigration Management and Refugee Recognition Law) that international students attend a minimum of 10 hours of classes per week. Since one class session lasts for an hour and a half at Ritsumeikan University, you must take at least 7 sessions of classes per week* in order to satisfy this requirement. At Ritsumeikan University, credits are calculated on the basis that one semester is 15 weeks long. For Japanese language courses, each weekly class session is accorded 1 credit each per semester. Courses other than Japanese courses are generally 2 credits each per semester. 獎學金資訊，請參考 此連結 [https://en.ritsumei.ac.jp/skp/academics/scholarships/] 。

年級限制: 大二以上(僅限大學部)學生
語言要求: TOEFL iBT 80 (讀 16 聽 16 說 16 寫 16 接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.5
學校年曆: Fall Term: September - December Winter Term: January - March Spring Term: March - June ✔ 該校年曆請參考 此連結 [https://registrar.oregonstate.edu/osu-academic-calendar] ✔ 交換一學年者為Fall Term, Winter Term及Spring Term， 第1學期交換者為Fall Term only，第2學期交換者為Spring Term only
註冊繳費: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔
accommodation_info: 不保證住宿 ，住宿資訊請參考 此連結 [https://uhds.oregonstate.edu/housing/international-student-information]
注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換學生資訊請參考 此連結 [https://internationalservices.oregonstate.edu/students/getting-here/exchange-students] 課程資訊：Look up courses by term in the OSU Schedule of Classes [https://classes.oregonstate.edu/] or review the archived schedules from past years [https://ir.library.oregonstate.edu/catalog?q=Oregon+State+University.+Registrar%26%2339%3Bs+Office&search_field=other_affiliation_label] . Find more detailed course information in the General Catalog [https://catalog.oregonstate.edu/courses/] . Master and PhD students: Students have to have received their bachelor's degree (or equivalent) before the OSU Graduate School will allow them to register for classes. They can be accepted while waiting for the official degree document, but they cannot register unless it is submitted first. Minimum academic workload per term: Undergraduate: 12 credits Graduate: 9 credits For Master's or PhD students, they are allowed to take undergraduate courses/subjects/units Some departments are more competitive than others so classes can fill up fast. Students should have adequate back up class options in many academic subjects if possible. MBA program is not available to exchange students. Students cannot take any course that awards a degree or certificate at completion. There are several academic subjects across campus that are popular or limited in size and are therefore difficult to place into classes. Students should have many alternative class options just in case their preferred classes are not available. Certificate programs are not open to exchange students. Our design program in the College of Business is also not open to exchange students because it is so small. Some classes might be unavailable if graduating degree seeking students are prioritized because they can fill quickly. Students can take language classes at OSU, but not as a way to strengthen their academic English (there is a program with academic English classes, but it its classes are not covered by the tuition waiver). Insurance Information [https://studenthealth.oregonstate.edu/insurance/international-student-insurance-plan] Estimated Costs [https://internationalservices.oregonstate.edu/ois/iess/estimated-expenses]

Once you arrive at UCL you will be expected to take the majority of your modules within this area. You can also choose modules from other subject areas, although be aware that this cannot be guaranteed – prerequisites may apply and spaces on certain modules may be limited.­­­­ Students must take courses that total 60 UCL credits per semester (30 ECTS/16 US Credits) which is typically 4 courses per semester and 120 credits/60 ECTS/32 US credits for the full year.

年級限制: 大二以上(僅限大學部)學生
語言要求: TOEFL iBT 79 (讀 19 聽 19 說 19 寫 19 不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.5 (聽 6.5 讀 6.5 寫 6.5 說 6.5)
不接受申請之學院: 醫學院、法律學院、社會科學院社會工作學系
學校年曆: Fall Semester: August - December Spring Semester: January - May ✔ 該校年曆請參考 此連結 [https://registrar.uconn.edu/academic-calendar/]
註冊繳費: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔
accommodation_info: 保證住宿 ，住宿資訊請參考 此連結 [http://reslife.uconn.edu] On-campus housing is guaranteed for all exchange students that complete the online housing application by the due date . We recommend that all exchange students live on campus in residence halls. On-campus living is an important part of American university life, and by living on campus, you become an active member of the UConn community and have access to all the social, recreational, and cultural events that take place. All dorm rooms are equipped with a bed, a mattress, a desk and chair, and a closet. In small units, doubles are 154 sq. ft. (11×14). Elsewhere, double rooms range in size up to 180 sq. ft. Most undergraduate dormitory rooms share bathroom facilities with other residents on the floor. Refrigerators, microwaves and televisions are not provided in dorm rooms. Traditional Housing – Shared bedrooms with convenient access to dining halls and campus facilities. A campus meal plan [http://dining.uconn.edu/meal-plans/] is required. Suites – Shared bedrooms, often with shared living spaces, with convenient access to dining halls and campus facilities. A campus meal plan [http://dining.uconn.edu/meal-plans/] is required. Apartments – Independent-style housing that has shared bedrooms and living spaces (including full kitchens). You are NOT required to have a campus meal plan.
注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換生資訊請參考 此連結 [https://app.studyabroad.uconn.edu/index.cfm?FuseAction=Programs.ViewProgramAngular&id=10296] 及 Fact Sheet [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/UConn_2025-2026-Fact-Sheet.pdf] 在具備相關領域之背景知識及選課標準的前提下，交換學生可跨領域選課。 As an exchange student you will be able to enroll in courses at the Storrs campus of the following areas of study: Schools and Colleges of Agriculture & Natural Resources- Engineering Liberal Arts & Sciences Business (courses are restricted to Business majors only) Limited course availability: Fine Arts, Statistics and Psychology Areas of Study Not Available: School of Education (EDCI, EGEN, EPSY, EDLR, EKIN), School of Nursing (NURS), School of Pharmacy (PHRX, PHAR, PHRM), School of Law (LAW) and Military Science (MISI) 課程資訊請參考 此連結 [https://egl.uconn.edu/courseregistration/] Course Load: Minimum: 12 credits (undergraduate) 9 credits (graduate) Maximum: 17 credits (no more than 5 courses) Course Registration: Students are required to submit their course preferences as part of the online application process. Upper-division courses (3000 level and above) require evidence of pre-requisites. Online course registration is not available for exchange students. Medical Insurance [https://international.global.uconn.edu/health-insurance-for-international-students/] : It is mandatory for all exchange students to have personal medical Insurance that meets all J-1 visa requirements.

年級限制: 大二以上/碩一以上(不含博士生)學生
語言要求: TOEFL iBT 79 (不接受 Home Edition) 或 IELTS 6.0；或具德語檢定成績 B2(中高級) 級以上
學校年曆: Academic Calendar [https://www.uni-stuttgart.de/en/study/application/academic-calendar/] (Semester 1 = winter semester, Semester 2 = summer semester) Winter semester: October 1 – March 31 Summer semester: April 1 – September 30
註冊繳費: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔
accommodation_info: The International Office can arrange housing for exchange students from overseas in student dorms on request. Further information on how to apply online for student housing will be emailed to successful applicants. The Studierendenwerk Stuttgart [https://www.studierendenwerk-stuttgart.de/en/accommodation/] is in charge of the allocation of the dormitory rooms. We do not have any influence concerning the location or the same price category of the allocated spaces. You will have to transfer the deposit of EUR 700 in advance . Otherwise, your dorm room will be cancelled. According to the regulations of the Studierendenwerk, students are required to book a minimum rental period : either from Sept. 1/March 1 (students who attend the intensive German course) or from Oct. 1/April 1 (students who do not attend the intensive German course) until Feb. 28/Aug. 31 (students who will stay for one semester). Students who will stay for two semesters will receive a rental contract for 11-12 months. Other periods of rental agreements are not possible! Only in the case of examinations in March or September can your residence contract be extended by one month after the examination registration period.

Courses in international MSc programs (taught in English) can only be taken on request. Prerequisite: You are in your 3rd or 4th year of study. English taught MSc programs: COMMAS, GEONENGINE, INFOTECH, ITECH, IUSD, MIP, PHYSICS, WASTE, WAREM, Chemical Engineering, Computational Linguistics, Computer Science, English and American Studies, Electrical Engineering and Material Science.

name_en: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
年級限制: 大二/大三/大四/碩一/碩二(不含博士生)學生
語言要求: 無
學校年曆: 第一學期：8月到1月 第二學期：2月到7月 校歷請參考 此連結 [https://ugs.hust.edu.cn/fwzq/xl.htm]
註冊繳費: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔
accommodation_info:
学费:根据两校协议,公费生免除接收学校的学费。
保险:交换生需在离境前自行于当地购买境外保险(含人身意外险,紧急救援险,住院医疗险),以涵盖交换期间一切医疗及意外支出,并将电子版发往或到校后交至我校。
住宿:校内住宿,本科宿舍为标准四人间配备,上床下桌,实现空调、热水器、独立卫生间全覆盖,设有洗衣房、自动
贩售机、咖啡机、直饮水等设施供学生日常使用。根据房型住宿费约为人民币500-700元/学期,床上用品需自行购买,
上网费,热水、电费等据实结算。
接送机服务:提供接送机服务,费用由我校承担。
所有课程以中文普通话教学
来校后交换生有机会参加丰富的交流活动，融入校园和当地
保險與生活費資訊
生活费：约人民币1000-1500元/月，依生活消费习惯各异
港澳台校際交流學生目前暫未納入基本醫療保險範圍，學生來校前應自行購買相關健康及意外傷害保險。
交换生需在离境前自行于当地购买境外保险（含人身意外险，紧急救援险，住院医疗险），以涵盖交换期间一切医疗及意外支出，并将电子版发往或到校后交至我校。

Cost of living: around US$400-600 a month During the semester, Unicamp offers the following classes: Portuguese for foreigners and Portuguese for Spanish speakers. In addition, only students who arrive in time for the placement interview (offered the week prior to the beginning of classes) are able to enroll. Classes are offered ONLY in Campinas.

International students are placed in home-stays located in the south zone of the city, the same as the university. Most families live near the university, within 30- 40 minutes distance from campus by city bus.
注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換生資訊請見 此連結 [https://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/students.html#exchange] 、Information Sheet請見 此連結 [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Fact-Sheet-CCCI-PUC-Rio2025.pdf] （僅供參考） 課程資訊請見 此連結 [https://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/study_english_puc.html] 、 此連結 [http://microhorario.rdc.puc-rio.br/WebMicroHorarioConsulta/MicroHorarioConsulta.aspx?sessao=U2lzdGVtYT1QVUNPTkxJTkVfQUxVTk8mQXBsaWNhY2FvPU1JQ1JPX0hPUkFSSU8mRnVuY2FvPUNPTlNVTFRBJklEPWE5ZGU3NzUyZTUzMjRmY2JhYWE1YzA2YjA4OTBkNDI3] 及 此連結 [https://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/download/BRAZIL%20and%20PUC%20Rio%20academic%20system%202019.pdf] Courses Available in English [http://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/study_english_puc.html] : Disciplines in English are not necessarily offered every semester. These courses are subject to academic planning. Exchange students must take a minimum of 2 classes/8 credits and a maximum of 5 classes/24 credits including the mandatory Portuguese as a second language course for those who are not proficient in Portuguese. Although students are not required a specific level of proficiency in Portuguese language, on the first day of classes all exchange students must take an oral placement test in order to confirm his/her knowledge of Portuguese. It is mandatory for all students not fully proficient in Portuguese to take a Portuguese as a Second Language course during the regular academic term according to his/her level. Students have the opportunity to take the Intensive Portuguese as a Second Language course before the regular semester begins, although this course is not mandatory. An administrative cost is charged to all exchange students. It is MANDATORY to all incoming students and CAN NOT BE WAIVED under any circunstance. More details about the payment, please access here [https://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/fees_tuition.html] . Students also have the opportunity to take an Intensive Portuguese as a Second Language course [http://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/portuguese.html] before the regular semester begins, although this course is not mandatory. Application Procedure [http://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/forms-hp_students.html] Cost of living information [https://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Rio-De-Janeiro] Visa application information [https://www.puc-rio.br/ensinopesq/ccci/incoming/visa.html] Insurance information: Students will have to send us by e-mail (scanned) the proof of your International Health Insurance Coverage for the entire duration of your stay in Brazil. Please include the insurance company’s name, policy number, dates of coverage and company contact information. Proof of Vaccination: COVID-19 Certificate of vaccination, Yellow fever immunization (recommended) International students are allowed to attend courses at PUC-Rio up to two regular terms plus the Pre-Term Portuguese as a second language Intensive Course.

注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換生資訊請見 此連結 [https://sri.uqam.ca/etudiants-uqam/echanges/nse-et-north2north/intro/] 、Fact Sheet 請見 此連結 [https://sri.uqam.ca/fact_sheet/] 課程資訊請見 此連結 [https://etudier.uqam.ca/programmes?gad=1&gclid=CjwKCAjws9ipBhB1EiwAccEi1CUgCwovmGp3b0K0UtJ4gN4N8S6YO2MvpWu-DxnVJCWjz3rm92ZFQBoCL6YQAvD_BwEhttps://etudier.uqam.ca/programmes?gad=1&gclid=CjwKCAjws9ipBhB1EiwAccEi1CUgCwovmGp3b0K0UtJ4gN4N8S6YO2MvpWu-DxnVJCWjz3rm92ZFQBoCL6YQAvD_BwE] 、French as a Second Language課程列表請見 此連結 [https://langues.uqam.ca/francais/] The Université du Québec à Montréal (UQAM) is a French-language university. Most courses are taught in French. A definitive admission to an exchange program does not guarantee access to the courses chosen by the student. Some courses may require the successful completion of prerequisite courses, may not be offered during your stay or may be full. You may have to choose other courses before or upon your arrival at UQAM. All courses identified as ateliers (workshop), stages (interships), Jeu (Acting) concentration, or séminaires d’intégration (integration seminar) are not available for exchange students. The majority of courses offered at UQAM are worth 3 credits. A 3-credit course corresponds to 45 class hours over 15 weeks at a rate of 3 hours per week. Double the hours for study and personal work. All exchange students must be enrolled in a full-time course load. The number of credits (per semester) required to be considered a full-time student: Undergraduate (Bachelor’s degree or Certificate): 12 to 15 credits (4 or 5 three-credit courses) Graduate levels (Master’s degree, DESS, PhD): 6 to 9 credits (2 or 3 three-credit courses) UQAM'S School of Management is not open to university-wide exchange students. Students are NOT allowed to take-cross faculty courses, and must stick to their home university major(s). For Faculty of Political Science and Law Baccalauréat en droit Minimum cumulative average required for the entire university curriculum: 3.4/4.3. Be enrolled in a law study program (law-economics-management courses are not eligible, unless transcripts show that the equivalent of 24 to 30 credits of law courses (48 - 60 ECTS) have bee successfully completed. Baccalauréat en relations internationales et droit international (BRIDI) Minimum cumulative average required for the entire university curriculum: 3.3/4.3. Be enrolled in a law or political science study program. For Faculty of Communication: Baccalauréat en communication (création médias - cinéma) The candidate must demonstrate a good knowledge of the theory and practice related to filmmaking in both documentary and fiction, as well as experience on set on at least two different film projects. First-year courses are not open to exchange students. In the second year, it is only possible to register for the winter term. Exchanges for a full year are only possible in the third year Maîtrise en communication At the graduate level, students can register for a maximum of 3 courses. No exceptions will be granted in the programs of the Faculty of Communication. For Faculty of Arts: Baccalauréat en art dramatique Concentration scénographie (7603) Concentration enseignement (7605) Concentration études théâtrales (6507) The Jeu (Acting) concentration is not open to exchange students. The number of places is limited. Some courses are not accessible to exchange students. Applicants must be enrolled in a similar program at the home institution. Depending on concentration, an interview process and/or submission of portfolio may be required. Estimated cost of living [https://etudier.uqam.ca/etudiants-internationaux/cout] : Living expenses for students, depending on their lodging and lifestyle, is about $1,500 CAD monthly. Health Insurance [https://portailetudiant.uqam.ca/international/assurances/] : Exchange students at UQAM are obliged to be covered by a health insurance plan ($356 CAD per semester).

Or,b) They find a Research Supervisor at METU (by themselves) who is ready to accept to study with the student. In this case, the student should submit an acceptance letter (which can be downloaded from the ICO website) signed by the agreed supervisor, department exchange coordinator, and department chair during the application period. Without this document, the student will not be able to conduct research at METU. Please note that it is not possible for ICO to assign academic supervisors to research students. Please also bear in mind that graduate students can study a part of their thesis, but they cannot submit the final version of the thesis and graduate from METU; those students are expected to present their thesis after their departure and graduate from their home institutions. International Joint-Degree Programs [https://www.metu.edu.tr/international-joint-degree-programs] are not open to exchange students. Students do not require an offer for Exchange or Study Abroad to apply for on-campus accommodation ; there is no fee to apply. Once applications open, students can go to the website to register, and complete an application. During the application process students must ensure they answer that they are a Monash Abroad applicant. As rooms are limited and offered on a first-come, first-served basis, we recommend students apply as soon as possible. Questions not answered on the website can be directed to Monash Residential Services [mailto:mrs.applications@monash.edu] . Off-campus accommodation If students choose to live off-campus, they can receive assistance/advice [https://www.monash.edu/accommodation/students/monash-university-preferred-student-accommodation] on searching for rental properties, setting up a share house, things to look out for before you sign a contract, an idea of comparable rental costs, how to budget, and how to get the essentials like internet, gas and electricity connected.

注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換學生資訊請參考 此連結 [https://internationaloffice.usp.br/en/index.php/admissions/studentexchange/exchange-student/] 、Fact Sheet請參考 此連結 [https://internationaloffice.usp.br/wp-content/uploads/docs/usp-fact-sheet.pdf] （僅供參考） 課程資訊請參考 此連結 [https://uspdigital.usp.br/mundus/listaDisciplinasInternacRedirect?codmnu=7414] 、語言課程請參考 此連結 [http://clinguas.fflch.usp.br/] 在具備相關領域之背景知識及選課標準的前提下，交換學生可跨領域選課，研究所學生可選修大學部課程。 Most courses at USP are taught exclusively in Portuguese. We strongly recommend that students have a good command of Portuguese to fully participate in classes (at least B1 level according to the CEFR). Master students willing to take courses only should apply preferably to the undergraduate level and for graduate courses upon arrival. Master and PhD students applying for a research placement should be nominated and apply by email. Special Requirements and Course Restrictions: The School of Engineering (Escola Politécnica) requires exchange students to enroll in a number of subjects that correspond to at least 12 credits. Ribeirão Preto School of Economics, Administration, and Accounting (Faculdade de Economia e Administração de Ribeirão Preto – FEARP) requires applicants to enroll in a number of subjects that correspond to at least 16 credits. The School of Architecture and Urbanism (Faculdade de Arquitetura e Urbanismo – FAU) and the School of Economics, Business and Accountancy (Faculdade de Economia, Administração e Contabilidade – FEA) receive each semester a large number of applications and have limited capacity to admit exchange students. We highly recommend that students intending to apply for such schools consider the possibility of alternatively applying for other schools as well. Minimum academic workload for exchange students depends on each School, but usually 12 credits per semester. Insurance: The student will also have to provide evidence of medical insurance or an appropriate and valid travel medical insurance policy for registration upon arrival. Students must purchase their Health/Travel Insurance in their countries of origin before travelling to Brazil (this must cover the entirety of the stay in Brazil). International students will have access to the Brazilian Public Health System only in emergency cases. USP has 8 campuses spread across the state of São Paulo. More information on each of USP’s campuses is available here [http://www.usp.br/internationaloffice/en/index.php/admissions/campus-guide] .

SURA created a policy that covers and exceeds all our minimum requirements.

Compulsory Insurance Plan: Students are required to purchase insurance and submit proof of insurance coverage prior to arrival. The insurance must cover travel, personal accidents, medical expenses (accident and non- accident), hospitalization and emergency evacuation/repatriation. Estimated Cost of Living: Accommodation: ranges from HK$1,900 to HK$2,300 per month, depend on the choice of the roomMeals: around HK$4,000 per month Personal Expenses: around HK$2,000 per month ( Actual expenses depend on your personal spending habit. ) All inbound exchange students are required to attend an Orientation which usually takes place a week prior to the commencement of the semester.

Exchange program is available only on Higashi-Osaka campus . Please be aware that Faculty of Agriculture, Faculty of Pharmacy, Faculty of Biology-Oriented Science and Technology, Faculty of Engineering, Faculty of Humanity-Oriente Science and Engineering are not open for exchange students. FACULTIES OFFERING COURSES TO EXCHANGE STUDENTS A= For Program A, courses in English B= For Program B, courses in Japanese-Faculty of Law （ B ） -Faculty of Economics （ B ） -Faculty of Business Administration （ A/B ） -Faculty of Science and Engineering （ B ） -Faculty of Architecture （ B ） -Faculty of Literature, Arts and Cultural Studies （ A/B ） -Faculty of Applied Sociology （ B ） -Faculty of International Studies （ A/B ） -Faculty of Informatics （ B ） -Global Education Center （ A/B ） Please note that graduate students can participate in our program but can register only for undergraduate level courses. Minimum academic workload for exchange students: 315 hours (45 hours/course x 7 courses Students have to be the ones who need credit transfer from at least 7 of our courses per semester while on exchange. The number of credits earned from our exchange program has to be counted as a part of the total number of credits that students need for graduation. In order to apply for our programs, please note all the following requirements need to be met: Those who have never studied in Japan before. Those who do not have Japanese nationality. Those who will not graduate or complete their degree program at their home institution before the end of the Kindai University exchange program. Students who do not currently take a leave of absent from their home institution Exchange students are not allowed to participate in club activities at Kindai University. Because the club activities insurance which is mandatory for club members to purchase is not available for exchange students, and the club members also require serious commitment throughout the year, aiming for winning national/world championship, whereas exchange students' priority is study.

注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換學生資訊請參考 此連結 [https://global.utexas.edu/isss/advising-services/student-exchange-program] 、 此連結 [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/ISSS_Smmr22_ExchangeStudentBrochure-1.pdf] 及 Fact Sheet [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/UT-Austin-Fact-Sheet_25-26.pdf] 、課程資訊請參考 此連結 [https://onestop.utexas.edu/registration-and-degree-planning/registering-for-classes/] 申請前請先閱讀 Understanding Fees for Exchange Students [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/UT-Austin-Required-Fees_25-26.pdf] Students who participate in the general exchange may select a major and take courses in the following UT Colleges and Schools: Cockrell School of Engineering Jackson School of Geosciences College of Education Moody College of Communication College of Fine Arts Steve Hicks School of Social Work College of Liberal Arts School of Undergraduate Studies College of Natural Sciences Academic Restrictions: Students admitted to the general or engineering exchange program may not enroll in business (see exception below) or law courses. To take courses in business or law, you must be admitted to the McCombs School of Business (M.B.A. or B.B.A.) or Law School exchange programs. The Computer Science program can only accommodate a limited number of exchange students. Students are welcome to apply to the program, but admission is not guaranteed and is subject to departmental review. Students accepted as Computer Science majors will be able to take no more than two Computer Science courses per semester. Students who are not admitted to the Computer Science major will not be allowed to enroll in Computer Science courses. The following programs are NOT open to exchange students: Dell Medical School Option III Programs School of Information (iSchool) Students who wish to attend the general exchange program in one of the following restricted majors will be initially admitted as an “undeclared” major. Once you have submitted all required admission materials, an Academic Advisor in the respective department will review your application, contact you directly if addition application materials are needed, and determine if admission to the major is possible. Computer Science (College of Natural Sciences) All Architecture Majors (School of Architecture) All Communication Majors (College of Communication) All Fine Arts Majors, except Art History (College of Fine Arts) All Music Majors (Butler School of Music) There are a limited number of courses for non-business majors that are open to students participating in the general exchange program. Undergraduate students must enroll in a minimum of 12 credits per semester during the academic year. Living expenses can vary from $1,600 to $2,000 per month depending on individual lifestyle. Health Insurance: [https://utx.global/student-insurance-plan] All reciprocal exchange students and any accompanying dependents (spouse/children) are required to have health insurance that meets both the U.S. Department of State and The University of Texas at Austin requirements. Enrollment in the UT Austin insurance plan is mandatory. Cost is approximately $1,500 USD per semester (subject to change).

accommodation_info: The University of Athens runs a Student Residence Complex (FEPA/ΦΕΠΑ in Greek) on campus consisting of 4 buildings (A, B, C, D), which can house 1064 students. In addition to student residences, there are various ways to find a flat in Athens, ranging from advertisements to agencies and digital services. Most apartments are unfurnished, while those near the city centre are usually old or recently renovated. Rent costs don't include public utility services (electricity, water, heating, telephone line, and internet). There is also a typical extra cost, a building fee , which is shared with the tenants, for services such as cleaners, gardeners, elevator maintenance, electricity for shared building spaces, etc. Renting an apartment requires signing a lease contract , which is necessary for establishing tenant rights and responsibilities. More information here [https://issu.uoa.gr/guide_for_all_students/getting_ready_for_your_studies/accommodation/] .

accommodation_info: 保證住宿 ，更多資訊請見 此連結 [https://ewww.kumamoto-u.ac.jp/en/prospective/accommodation/international_house/] 。 Housing is guaranteed (International House, the dormitory). You may rent a public apartment [https://ewww.kumamoto-u.ac.jp/en/prospective/accommodation/others/] if you wish. Exchange students from partner schools are not required to live on campus and may find their own accommodations in the area, but should be aware that finding off-campus housing, especially for only one semester, can be challenging. Spots in campus housing are reserved for exchange students.
注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力為該校之最低門檻要求，同學需自行確認語言能力是否符合該校修課要求、查詢是否有相對應系所及足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識，若未達標準，該校有權拒收 交換學生計畫（Short-Term Exchange Programme, ‘STEP’ ）資訊請參考 此連結 [https://ewww.kumamoto-u.ac.jp/en/prospective/undergraduate/exchange/] 、 Fact Sheet [https://studyabroad.ntu.edu.tw/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Fact-Sheet-Kumamoto-University_2025-26rev.pdf] （僅供參考）及 此連結 [https://ewww.kumamoto-u.ac.jp/media/medialibrary/2022/02/ShortTermExchangeProgramBrochure.pdf] 。 Language of Instruction : Mostly Japanese , some subjects in English. J Course (J コース): Students in this course mainly take classes in specialised subjects for undergraduate students of each faculty, as well as Japanese Language classes. Also, they can take general education classes taught in English. E Course (E コース): Students in this course mainly take STEP classes taught in English and the Japanese Language classes provided in Japanese. If you wish to take other classes, you must have a consultation with the office staff in advance. Also, those classes will NTO be calculated towards programme completion. Faculty open for Short-Term Exchange Students: Faculty of Letters Faculty of Law Faculty of Education Faculty of Science Faculty of Engineering Faculty NOT open for Short-Term Exchange Students: School of Medicine School of Pharmacy Japanese Language Classes are limited to 7 credits per semester. Students must take at least 7 classes per week and 10 credits each semester . To take General Education classes and Specialised classes, Japanese language level more than N2 of JLPT is the criterion. Also, not all the classes are available for STEP students. Addtionally, you may not be able to take those courses if the language level is under the criterion.

UC3M Residence Halls Information [https://colegiosmayores.fund.uc3m.es/en/] 2) Private Residence Halls : Not affiliated with the UC3M but still very close to the Campus and already in use by many international partners. Getafe: LIV Student Getafe [https://www.livensaliving.com/residencias-estudiantes-madrid/residencia-estudiantes-madrid-getafe/] MiCampus Getafe [https://micampusresidencias.com/residencias-universitarias-madrid/micampus-getafe/] Amro Getafe [https://amroestudiantes.es/getafe-es/] Leganes: MiCampus Leganés [https://micampusresidencias.com/micampus-leganes/] 3) Housing agencies : The ones listed on our webpage are the ones the University has signed collaboration agreements with, so these are the ones we strongly recommend using, in case the students want to use their services to share an apartment in Madrid, Getafe, or Leganes, or request options other than living in a residence hall: UC3M Housing Agencies [https://www.uc3m.es/ss/Satellite/ApoyoEstudiante/en/TextoDosColumnas/1371376076941/Alojamiento]

年級限制: 大二以上/碩一以上(不含博士生)學生

學校年曆: 該校年曆請參考 此連結 [https://en.knu.ac.kr/edu/academic01.htm] Fall Semester: September - December Spring Semester: March - June
註冊繳費: 須在本校註冊並繳交全額學雜費，免繳該校學費，但該校雜費、住宿費、生活費、保險費、往返機票費等均須自行負擔
accommodation_info: Students can apply for on-campus housing to be assigned to a shared room in one of several on-campus dormitories. The dormitory fee for one semester can range from around USD $500 to $1,000, depending on the meal plan (which is optional). This application should be completed separately by online application before the start of the semester.
注意事項: 交換學校一覽表所列之語言能力等規定為該校之最低門檻，同學需自行確認最新資訊，如語言能力及成績是否符合該校要求、查詢是否有足夠的課程可供選讀、能否達到該校之每學期修課學分數要求、以及是否具備相關領域之背景知識等，若未達標準，該校有權拒收。本表所提供之資訊如有與交換學校／國家／地區最新公告不符之處，申請學生應以交換學校／國家／地區之最新公告為準。 經甄選錄取獲得本校推薦資格之交換生，不得於取得該校入學許可後，要求變更已取得的入學許可條件；或者是交換申請被該校拒絕後，要求以另一種條件重新提出申請。如有以上情事者，將直接取消其錄取資格。 交換資訊請參考 網頁 [https://en.knu.ac.kr/admission/exchange01.htm#con08] 及 Factsheet [https://en.knu.ac.kr/download/241010_knufactsheet.pdf] 。 The normal course load for students at KNU is 5-6 courses (which is usually 18 Korean credits). Most KNU classes are three-credit courses and in order to get three credits in KNU, students have to complete 45 hours of studies (3 hours × 15 weeks). (1 course = 3 US credits, 45 class hours per semester, including exam period) Undergraduate: Undergraduate students can take a maximum of 18 credits (6 courses) per semester, and the minimum requirement is 6 credits (2 courses per semester.) Graduate: Master’s students can take a maximum of 10 Korean credits, which is usually 3 courses. Each semester's course lists are finalized in July and January, and updated on the KNU website ( https://en.knu.ac.kr/admission/exchange01.htm [https://en.knu.ac.kr/admission/exchange01.htm] ) . Students should check the course list to prepare to register for classes between the given period in early August (Fall Semester) and early February (Spring Semester). Medicine, dentistry, nursing, pharmacy, law, art, music, and teaching is limited to international exchange students
